narrative: FY2022-23 shows a broad leadership cluster rather than a single leader. Tata Steel, JSW Steel and Jindal Stainless reported 100% coverage across employee H&S, employee skill, worker H&S and worker skill-upgradation. SAIL matched the leaders only on worker H&S at 100%, but lagged on employee H&S at 30.4%, employee skill at 32.6%, and did not report worker skill-upgradation. Tata’s FY2022-23 figures are flagged as consolidated, so the 100% coverage includes a wider operating boundary than standalone Indian operations; this makes Tata’s result strong, but not fully like-for-like against standalone peers. The strategic signal is that Tata and JSW appear to have institutionalised training coverage as a system metric, while SAIL’s strength was more concentrated in worker H&S, consistent with safety-critical integrated steel operations and contractor risk management. Jindal’s 100% across all four metrics in FY2022-23 also indicates robust coverage before the FY2023-24 dip.
